ground_truth.title: What happened with Arch Linux ISO in 2024
ground_truth.author: chunix64
ground_truth.main_content:
chunix64
2025-11-01 15:14:37

According to https://archlinux.org/releng/releases/ , why did the ISO size increase so rapidly from 2024 to 2025 — from about 800 MB to 1.4 GB, almost half the total size growth in Arch's entire history?

Last edited by chunix64 (2025-11-01 15:15:18)

nl6720
2025-11-02 06:23:21

Re: What happened with Arch Linux ISO in 2024

archlinux-2024.05.01-x86_64.iso changed the UEFI boot loader from GRUB back to systemd-boot (which was used in archlinux-2022.06.01-x86_64.iso and prior ISOs) which means the kernel and initramfs need to be duplicated; see https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/archiso/-/merge_requests/374 for details. The rest of the size increase is just general bloat accumulation. I.e. the kernel and linux-firmware get bigger with each release.
with: why did the ISO size increase so rapidly from 2024 to 2025
from about 800 MB to 1.4 GB, almost half the total size growth in Arch's entire history
archlinux-2024.05.01-x86_64.iso changed the UEFI boot loader from GRUB back to systemd-boot
which means the kernel and initramfs need to be duplicated
The rest of the size increase is just general bloat accumulation
